calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-30-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SUBSTACK INC.'s Motion To Vacate Default Judgment.

No other appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Brian Collins, acting in pro per, failed to file his opposition papers with this court. Defendant Substack has provided a copy with its reply brief. The court exercises its discretion to consider the arguments in opposition made by Collins and grants Substack's motion to vacate the default judgment.

Substack challenges the personal and subject-matter jurisdiction of the New Hampshire court that rendered a sister-state default judgment against it.

A default judgment may be vacated where the court lacked personal jurisdiction over the defendant, including where the defendant was not served with process. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process, the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413 [internal quotation marks, citation, and footnote omitted].) Similarly, "[w]hen a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction [on the basis of lack of contacts with the forum state], the plaintiff has the initial burden of 'demonstrating facts justifying the exercise of jurisdiction.' " (Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1250 [quoting Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 449.)

Here, Substack challenges both service and the New Hampshire court's exercise of personal jurisdiction over it, placing the burden on Collins to provide evidence (beyond conclusory assertions) of service and jurisdiction. Collins has presented no competent evidence to carry that burden. The court therefore grants Substack's motion and vacates the judgment pursuant to CCP 1710.25 entered by this court on March 5, 2024.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
